title: Ekteshwar Temple
tagline: Ekteshwar Temple — spiritual town of West Bengal

overview.short: Ekteswar temple is a Hindu temple dedicated to Shiva, is located at Ekteswar, Bankura district, in West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Ekteswar
topPlaces.description: Ekteswar was earlier a village and now a part of the outskirts of Bankura town in Bankura district, in the Indian state of West Bengal. It is situated on the bank of Dwarakeswar River.

topPlaces.name: Sonatapal
topPlaces.description: Sonatapal is located 3.2 kilometres (2.0 mi) north-east of Ekteswar, near Bankura town, on the banks of Dwarakeswar River in Bankura district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Agaya
topPlaces.description: Agaya is a village in Bankura I Sub District in Bankura district, West Bengal, India.

topPlaces.name: Bankura Zilla Saradamani Mahila Mahavidyapith
topPlaces.description: Bankura Zilla Saradamani Mahila Mahavidyapith, established in 1973, is a women's college in Bankura district of West Bengal, India. It is the only women's college in Bankura district.

topPlaces.name: Bankura I
topPlaces.description: Bankura I is a community development block (CD block) that forms an administrative division in the Bankura Sadar subdivision of the Bankura district in the Indian state of West Bengal.

topPlaces.name: Rarh region
topPlaces.description: Rarh region (Bengali pronunciation: [raːɽʱ]) is a toponym for an area in the Indian subcontinent that lies between the Chota Nagpur Plateau on the West and the Ganges Delta on the East. Although the boundaries of the region have been defined differently according to various sources throughout history, it is mainly coextensive with the state of West Bengal, also comprising parts of the state of Jharkhand in India.

topPlaces.name: Bankura
topPlaces.description: Bankura () is a city and a municipality in the state of West Bengal, India. It is the headquarters of the Bankura district.

topPlaces.name: Bikna
topPlaces.description: Bikna is a village in the Bankura II CD block in the Bankura Sadar subdivision of the Bankura district in the state of West Bengal, India.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Ekteshwar Temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Ekteshwar Temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Ekteshwar Temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose International Airport, Kolkata (152 km) and the nearest railway station is Dhanbad Junction (93 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Ekteshwar Temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Ekteshwar Temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Ekteshwar Temple?
faq.a: Stays in Ekteshwar Temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Ekteshwar Temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ekteswar, Sonatapal, Agaya, Bankura Zilla Saradamani Mahila Mahavidyapith, Bankura I.
